MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-30-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. INTERVENOR NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT is denied. First, NASIC fails to maintain its burden of production. NASIC relies on paragraph 7 of the declaration of counsel (Mr. Kim) for the proposition that the developer transferred all of its assets to plaintiff. The declaration does not support that assertion and the most that Mr. Kim shows is that the developer "created" plaintiff. Defendant does not show that the developer's assets and liabilities were transferred to plaintiff or that plaintiff is the successor in interest of the developer. NASIC therefore fails to show that plaintiff is subject to the cited contract. Second, the declarations of Mr. Bartak (board member of plaintiff) and Mr. Santana (member of developer) state that the developer never transferred any assets or liabilities to plaintiff. Those declarations create a triable issue of material fact regarding whether plaintiff is subject to the contract. The court denies the sanctions request. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Cross-Complaint Jointly Filed By Santana Real Estate Development Esquivel Santana
Off Calendar Dec-18-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. PAUL H. KIM of Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe, Tel No. (510) 834-4350 appearing for Intervenor North American Specialty Insurance Company;
Atty. ROBERT P. GARCIA, Tel No. (415) 648-3060 appearing for Cross-Complainants Santana Real Estate Development, LLC and Esquivel Santana.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. INTERVENOR NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against the Cross-Complaint Jointly Filed By Santana Real Estate Development, LLC and Esquivel Santana is GRANTED. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that the developer (Santana Real Estate Development, LLC) and guarantor (Esquivel Santana) agreed to hold harmless the general contractor (Matrix Construction). (See Par. 9 of Addendum 1 and Guaranty). Matrix Construction's insurer (moving party North American Specialty Insurance Company) is entitled to rely on that covenant and is accordingly entitled to summary judgment. See Reliance Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 383, 387-388. The court overrules the opposing parties' evidentiary objections. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

Off Calendar Dec-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MONICA MONROE, ESQ., 415.617.2210, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. INTERVENOR NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Costs Claimed Defendant In Intervention Otis Elevator Company is denied. Cross-defendant Otis Elevator Co. is entitled to its costs from Matrix as a matter of right. A cross-defendant obtained a dismissal. CCP 1032(a)(4) specifies that cross-defendant is a prevailing party in such circumstances. See Great W. Bank v. Converse Consultants, Inc. (1997) 58 CalApp.4th 609, 612 ("[C]osts are available as a matter of right to a cross-defendant in whose favor a dismissal is entered."). Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
